description: 29 - 13-09-2025 - Emails between Ailsa Terry (Private Secretary for Foreign Affairs, No.10), Sir  Oliver Robbins (former Permanent Under-Secretary of State for Foreign Affairs, FCDO) and others  Subje

participants.name: Sir  Oliver Robbins
participants.role: former Permanent Under-Secretary of State for Foreign Affairs

text:
Oliver Robbins (former Permanent Under-Secretary of State for Foreign Affairs, FCDO) and others 
Subject: RE: FAC letter re HMA Washington  
Date: Saturday, 13 September 2025 at 14:02:10 British Summer Time 
From: Caroline Hurndall  
To: Oliver Robbins, Dan York-Smith, Ailsa Terry  
CC: Serena Stone, 
 Mike Ostheimer  
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE 
Ailsa,  
Can I also raise a similar issue with regards to the development and handling of press 
lines and background briefing on these issues. Our comms team have been told that 
No10 SpAds are handling the timeline issues; on thisand on continued conversations , 
on vetting, it’s very important for accuracy sake that our team have chance to check 
those lines before they are used, including on background. We’re keen to stay in 
lockstep and be speaking with one voice. Could you please encourage your Spads 
and comms colleagues to stay in close touch with their oppos?  
Thank you,  
Caroline  
Caroline Hurndall│PPS/FS │ 
The Foreign Secretary’s box deadline is midday, Monday to Thursday. Please allow a 
minimum of 48hrs to process work sent to FSPO. Please also copy 
 to 
all submissions/updates to the Foreign Secretary.  
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE 
From: Oliver Robbins  
Sent: 13 September 2025 13:09  
To: Caroline Hurndall ; Dan York-Smith ; Ailsa Terry (No.10) 
Cc: Serena Stone ; 
 Mike Ostheimer  
Subject: Subject: Re: FAC letter re HMA Washington  
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE  
Ailsa  
I can understand why people are interested in questions like these, but I think we 
need to be very careful about sharing such information. If this ends in court, this 
department will be the defendant.  
I will speak to Dan about making sure we are keeping the creation of new 
disclosable evidence within reasonable bounds.  
Olly  
—  
Oliver Robbins  
FCDO  
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE 
Personal
JCS
JCS
JCS
80

From: Ailsa Terry 
Sent: Saturday, September 13, 2025 1:03:07 PM  
To: Caroline Hurndall 
; Dan York-Smith 
 Oliver Robbins 
Cc: Serena Stone 
; 
Mike Ostheimer 
 Subject: Subject: Re: FAC letter re HMA 
Washington  
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE 
Dear Caroline, 
This sounds right to us. We will need to be definitive / clear that Ministers and No 10 
are not involved in the vetting process in anyway.  
May I ask also that the PUS' office supply two further bits of information asap for 
our timeline as we continue to refine it to ensure total completeness:  
when the COI forms were returned from Peter to FCDO 
when the DV process took place and was completed (forgive me if this 
was already provided)  
Many thanks,  
Ailsa  
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE 
From: Caroline Hurndall <
Sent: Saturday, September 13, 2025 10:15 am  
To: Dan York-Smith 
; Ailsa Terry 
Oliver Robbins 
Cc: Serena Stone 
Subject: Subject: Subject: Re: FAC letter re HMA Washington  
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE 
Dan,  
Thanks for this. Team are drafting as we speak; Ailsa and I agreed early afternoon for 
a draft. I’ve asked for it to be based on - and not go further than - our public lines on 
vetting, and then to add some lines on appointment processes in order to gently push 
back on the idea that the FAC might be able to assist/assure our recruitment 
processes. Finally, it would include some lines referring to the fact that HMA Washington 
met the Foreign Affairs Committee on their visit to Washington DC in June 2025; further 
meeting was planned when HMA was in London later in June but had to be postponed due to 
scheduling changes.  
Do let me know if you think that sounds along the right lines, draft to 
follow. Best wishes  
Caroline  
Sent from Outlook for iOS  
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE 
From: Dan York-Smith 
Sent: Saturday, September 13, 2025 9:50:29 AM 
Personal

To: Ailsa Terry (No.10) 
; Caroline Hurndall 
; Oliver Robbins 
Cc: Serena Stone 
Subject: Subject: RE: FAC letter re HMA Washington  
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE 
Morning – just checking in on when we might be able to see a draft. Will reassure 
people here to know how we can answer.  
Thanks  
Dan  
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE 
From: Ailsa Terry
Sent: 12 September 2025 19:21  
To: Caroline Hurndall
 Oliver 
Robbins 
Cc: Serena Stone 
Dan York-Smith 
Subject: Subject: Subject: FAC letter re HMA Washington  
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE 
Hi both  
As discussed briefly with Caroline, we would be grateful for sight of a draft reply to the 
letter Emily Thornberry has just sent to the FS asap - ideally tomorrow morning. To 
reassure this is not for immediate release and appreciate you need to run it past the FS, 
but we are keen it is answered ahead of any parliamentary engagements next week. 
We also need a line for comms tonight which I know our teams are working together on. 
Ailsa  
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE

parsing_notes: Extract structured email headers (From, To, Cc, Subject, Date/Sent) for each message in the chain. Identify reply/forward nesting depth. Parse each message body separately. Redacted addresses appear as 'PERSONAL' — names are retained. Build sender→recipient edges per message for communication graph.
